title: Documenting feature decisions
scope: Feeds Appendix C (feature documentation template). Discusses why engineering a feature requires logging its origin, logic, and evaluation results for long-term maintainability.
key_concepts: An undocumented feature becomes a liability because the engineering team will not know if it is safe to alter or remove later.
Documentation should capture the business logic, the exact mathematical transformation, the source data, and the initial baseline impact.
Good logging prevents duplicate engineering effort and allows new team members to trace complex derivations.
examples: Recording that 'ratio_income_debt' was created because raw debt alone produced poor separation across income brackets, and it improved the validation AUC by 0.04.
relations_to_other_chapters: Directly provides the foundation for the Feature Documentation Template in Appendix C.
what_to_exclude: Instructions on setting up automated data catalogs like Amundsen or Datahub.
